propositions: Actions in release.yml are referenced by mutable major-version tags rather than full commit SHAs, so identical rebar SHAs can run different code across runs.
Release checkouts do not set persist-credentials: false, leaving the token persisted in local git config.
The MCP publisher is downloaded from a mutable releases/latest URL and executed without digest/signature verification inside a job holding id-token: write.
Build tools install with unrestricted upgrades and no hash-locked constraints, allowing free dependency resolution during a production release.
Generic action-security checks (zizmor, actionlint) are not enforced on release.yml under the Gerrit make lint gate.
The OIDC-privileged publish work must be structurally split into an unprivileged mcp_verify job and an OIDC mcp_registry job so the privileged scope is never co-resident with an unverified binary.
